topic_title: For loops — repeating an action across a list

source_preflight.detail: 5 nodes: marks list (input), more items? (decision), loop body executes/total += mark (body), advance to next item (iteration), loop ends/total = 251 (terminal). All labels concise ≤4 words or compound-noun. Every node maps to corpus §5.2 (for loop anatomy) and §5.4 (accumulator). Loop-back edge D→B correctly models iteration cycle.

visual_inspection.detail: TB layout correct for loop control flow diagram. Input node blue (#a5d8ff), decision diamond yellow (#fff3bf), loop body + advance nodes purple (#d0bfff), terminal green (#b2f2bb). Loop-back arrow from 'advance to next item' curves cleanly back to 'more items?' on the right side — legible, no node overlap. True/False edge labels positioned clearly. Title 'for loop — iteration control flow' readable at top.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 5-node TB flowchart cleanly illustrates for loop iteration cycle: input list → decision diamond → body (accumulator) → advance → loop back or exit. Color tiers correct. Loop-back arrow legible. No visual defects.
